title: Zhuangzi, Chapter 12: Heaven and Earth
description: Chapter 12 of the Zhuangzi — the Taoist masterpiece of paradox and parable.
introduction: Chapter 12 of the Zhuangzi — the Taoist masterpiece of paradox and parable.

translation.text: In the great beginning there was non-being. There was no being, no name. From this came the One. There was the One, but there was no form. When things obtained it and came to life, it was called virtue. When things had no form yet were divided, this was called fate.
